info.title: GorillaFileSystem
info.description: GorillaFileSystem provides a virtual file system interface supporting typical file and directory operations such as navigation, creation, modification, and inspection within a hierarchical structure rooted at a designated root directory. It maintains a current working directory state initialized from the root upon loading a scenario, which defines the file system layout. Paths are generally relative to the current directory, and operations restrict file and directory names to exclude certain special characters. The API returns results or error messages in dictionary form, consistently indicating success or failure. It supports recursive directory loading from structured input and offers utilities for content manipulation and querying, all while enforcing local directory context for file operations to ensure controlled and predictable file system interactions.

behavior_hints: Listing returns direct children of the current scope only and does not recurse into nested directories.
Dot-prefixed hidden names are excluded only when the hide/filter flag is false; when enabled, hidden names in the current scope are included.

result.error: f'cd: {folder}: No such directory. You cannot use path to change directory.'
Cuurent directory is already the root. Cannot go back.
f"mkdir: cannot create directory '{dir_name}': Invalid character"
f"mkdir: cannot create directory '{dir_name}': File exists"

behavior_hints: Pattern matching is performed against splitlines() output and each returned entry must be the full original line verbatim (no substring extraction, no trimming, no normalization).

result.error: f"mv: cannot move '{source}': No such file or directory"
f"mv: cannot move '{source}': Not a file or directory"
f'mv: no path allowed in destination. Only file name and folder name is supported for this operation.'
f"mv: cannot move '{source}' to '{destination}/{source}': File exists"

behavior_hints: Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{source}' moved to '{destination}'
Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{source}' moved to '{destination}/{source}'
Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{file_name}' removed

result.error: f"rmdir: failed to remove '{dir_name}': Directory not empty"

behavior_hints: Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{dir_name}' removed

result.error: f"cp: cannot copy '{source}': No such file or directory"
f"cp: cannot copy '{source}': Not a file or directory"
f"cp: don't allow path in destination. Only file name and folder name is supported for this operation."
f"cp: cannot copy '{source}' to '{destination}/{source}': File exists"

behavior_hints: Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{source}' copied to '{destination}'
Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{source}' copied to '{destination}/{source}'

post.description: Returns the absolute path of the current working directory within the virtual file system. The path is constructed from the root directory to the current directory state, reflecting any prior navigation changes.

current_working_directory.description: Absolute path of the current working directory starting with '/'

post.summary: List contents of the current directory
post.description: Returns the direct children of the current directory without recursion. By default, hidden files and directories (names starting with a dot) are excluded unless the 'a' flag is set to true, which includes them in the listing. Behavior constraints: Listing returns direct children of the current scope only and does not recurse into nested directories. Dot-prefixed hidden names are excluded only when the hide/filter flag is false; when enabled, hidden names in the current scope are included.

a.description: If true, include hidden files and directories (those starting with a dot) in the listing; otherwise, exclude them.

current_directory_content.description: List of names of files and directories directly under the current directory, filtered by the 'a' flag.

post.summary: Change the current working directory to a specified folder.
post.description: Changes the current directory to the given folder name, which must be a single directory name and not a path. Supports moving up one level with '..' unless already at the root. Returns the new current working directory name on success or an error if the folder does not exist or navigation is invalid.

folder.description: The target folder name to change into; only one directory level allowed, or '..' to move up.

current_working_directory.description: The name of the new current working directory after the change.

post.summary: Create a new directory in the current directory
post.description: Creates a new directory with the specified name inside the current working directory. Directory names must not contain invalid characters and must not already exist in the current directory. On success, the directory is added to the current directory's contents.

dir_name.description: The name of the new directory to create in the current directory; must be unique and valid.

oneOf.description: Indicates successful directory creation with no additional data.

post.summary: Create a new empty file in the current directory.
post.description: Creates a new file with the specified name in the current working directory. The file name must be local (no path allowed) and must not contain invalid characters. If a file or directory with the same name already exists, the operation fails. On success, the file system state is updated with the new empty file.

file_name.description: The name of the new file to create in the current directory; must be a valid local file name without path components.

oneOf.description: Indicates successful creation of the file with no additional data returned.

post.summary: Write content to a file or display it in the terminal.
post.description: Writes the provided content to a specified file in the current directory if a file name is given, creating the file if it does not exist or overwriting it if it does. If no file name is provided, the content is returned as terminal output without modifying the file system. File names must be valid and exclude special characters to be accepted.

content.description: The content to write to the file or display in the terminal.

file_name.description: Optional name of the file in the current directory to write the content to; if omitted, content is displayed in the terminal.

terminal_output.description: The content displayed in the terminal when no file name is provided.

oneOf.description: Success response containing terminal output when no file is written.

post.summary: Display the contents of a file in the current directory.
post.description: Reads and returns the full content of a specified file located in the current working directory. The file name must not contain invalid characters and cannot include any path components. If the specified name corresponds to a directory or does not exist, the operation fails and returns an error.

file_name.description: The name of the file in the current directory to display. Must be a valid file name without path separators.

oneOf.description: Success response containing the full content of the requested file.

post.summary: Recursively search for files and directories by name under a given path.
post.description: Performs a recursive search starting from the specified directory path, returning all files and directories whose names contain the given substring. If no name is provided, all items under the path and its subdirectories are returned. Paths in the result are relative to the specified search path.

path.description: The directory path to start the recursive search from; defaults to current directory if omitted.

name.description: Optional substring to filter file and directory names; if omitted, all items are matched.

matches.description: List of relative paths to files and directories matching the search criteria.

post.summary: Count lines, words, or characters in a file.
post.description: Counts the number of lines, words, or characters in a specified file within the current directory. The mode parameter controls which unit is counted: lines ('l'), words ('w'), or characters ('c'). Returns the count and the type of unit counted. If the file does not exist or mode is invalid, an error is returned.

file_name.description: Name of the file in the current directory to count units from.

mode.description: Counting mode: 'l' for lines, 'w' for words, 'c' for characters.

count.description: The number of lines, words, or characters counted.

post.summary: Sort file contents line by line
post.description: Reads the specified file from the current directory and returns its contents sorted line by line in ascending order. The operation only succeeds if the file exists and is accessible in the current directory context. No changes are made to the file system state.

file_name.description: The name of the file in the current directory whose contents will be sorted.

sorted_content.description: The content of the file sorted line by line in ascending order.

post.summary: Search lines containing a pattern in a file at current directory
post.description: Searches for lines in the specified file within the current directory that contain the given pattern. Matching is performed on each line as split by line breaks, returning full lines exactly as they appear without trimming or modification. The file must exist locally in the current directory; no path traversal is allowed. Behavior constraints: Pattern matching is performed against splitlines() output and each returned entry must be the full original line verbatim (no substring extraction, no trimming, no normalization).

file_name.description: Name of the file in the current directory to search within; paths are not permitted.

pattern.description: Substring pattern to search for in each line of the file.

matching_lines.description: List of full lines from the file that contain the pattern.

post.summary: Estimate disk usage of current directory
post.description: Calculates the total size of the current directory and all its contents recursively. If human_readable is true, the size is formatted with appropriate units (B, KB, MB, etc.); otherwise, it is returned as a byte count string. The operation always reflects the current directory context and its nested files and directories.

human_readable.description: If true, returns the size in a human-readable format such as KB or MB; otherwise, returns size in bytes.

disk_usage.description: The estimated disk usage of the current directory and its contents, formatted as a string.

post.summary: Display the last lines of a file in the current directory.
post.description: Returns the last specified number of lines from a file located in the current directory. If the requested number of lines exceeds the file length, the entire file content is returned. File paths are not allowed; only files in the local directory can be accessed.

file_name.description: The name of the file in the current directory to read from; no path allowed.

lines.description: The number of lines to display from the end of the file; defaults to 10 if not specified.

last_lines.description: The last part of the file content as a single string with lines separated by newline characters.

oneOf.description: Success response containing the last lines of the file.

post.summary: Compare two files line by line in the current directory.
post.description: Compares the contents of two files line by line within the current directory. Returns a string highlighting differing lines with '-' and '+' prefixes. Both files must exist and be regular files; otherwise, an error is returned.

file_name1.description: The name of the first file in the current directory to compare.

file_name2.description: The name of the second file in the current directory to compare.

diff_lines.description: A string showing line-by-line differences between the two files, with lines removed from the first file prefixed by '-' and lines added in the second file prefixed by '+'.

post.summary: Move or rename a file or directory within the current directory.
post.description: Moves a file or directory named 'source' to a new location or name 'destination' within the current directory. If 'destination' is an existing directory, the source is moved inside it unless a name conflict occurs. If 'destination' is not an existing directory, the source is renamed to 'destination'. Paths are not allowed in 'destination', and both source and destination must be local to the current directory. Behavior constraints: Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{source}' moved to '{destination}' Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{source}' moved to '{destination}/{source}'

source.description: Name of the file or directory to move; must exist in the current directory.

destination.description: Target name or directory within the current directory to move or rename the source to; cannot contain path separators.

result.description: Confirmation message indicating the source was moved to the destination or into the destination directory. Expected format template: '{source}' moved to '{destination}' or '{source}' moved to '{destination}/{source}' Expected format template: '{source}' moved to '{destination}' or '{source}' moved to '{destination}/{source}'

post.summary: Remove a file or directory from the current directory.
post.description: Deletes the specified file or directory from the current working directory if it exists. The operation only succeeds if the target is a valid file or directory within the current directory context. On success, returns a confirmation message indicating the removed item. Behavior constraints: Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{file_name}' removed

file_name.description: The name of the file or directory to remove from the current directory.

result.description: Confirmation message indicating the removed file or directory. Expected format template: '{file_name}' removed Expected format template: '{file_name}' removed

post.summary: Remove an empty directory in the current directory
post.description: Deletes a directory named by 'dir_name' within the current directory if it exists and is empty. The operation fails if the directory does not exist, is not a directory, or contains any files or subdirectories. On success, returns a confirmation message indicating the directory was removed. Behavior constraints: Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{dir_name}' removed

dir_name.description: The name of the directory to remove; must be local to the current directory and empty.

result.description: Confirmation message that the directory was removed, formatted as '{dir_name} removed'. Expected format template: '{dir_name}' removed Expected format template: '{dir_name}' removed

post.summary: Copy a file or directory within the current directory.
post.description: Copies a file or directory named 'source' to a new location 'destination' within the current directory. If 'destination' is an existing directory, the source is copied inside it under the same name. Both source and destination must be local names without path separators. The operation fails if the source does not exist, the destination path is invalid, or if copying would overwrite existing content. Behavior constraints: Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{source}' copied to '{destination}' Success field 'result' uses formatted template: '{source}' copied to '{destination}/{source}'

source.description: Name of the file or directory to copy from the current directory.

destination.description: Name of the target file or directory within the current directory. If it matches an existing directory, source is copied inside it.

result.description: Confirmation message indicating the source was copied to the destination or into the destination directory. Expected format template: '{source}' copied to '{destination}' or '{source}' copied to '{destination}/{source}' Expected format template: '{source}' copied to '{destination}' or '{source}' copied to '{destination}/{source}'
